entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures, often low in nitrogen.

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: An essential nutrient for plant growth, often limiting in semi-arid soils.

entities.name: MILLET
entities.description: Cereal crops grown in semi-arid tropics.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Cereal crop grown in semi-arid tropics.

entities.name: FERTILISER
entities.description: External inputs used to provide nutrients to crops.

entities.name: LEGUME
entities.description: Plants capable of symbiotic nitrogen fixation, noted as having a small role in soil fertility maintenance in this context.

entities.name: SOIL FERTILITY
entities.description: The ability of soil to sustain plant growth through nutrient availability.

entities.name: GRASSES
entities.description: Vegetation including species like Andropogon goyonus.

entities.name: ANDROPOGON GOYONUS
entities.description: A specific grass species that allows nitrogen accumulation in soil fallows.

entities.name: MINERALISATION
entities.description: The process of converting organic nitrogen into inorganic forms like nitrate.

entities.name: NITRATE
entities.description: An inorganic form of nitrogen available for plant uptake.

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A specific type of millet crop that removes nitrogen from the soil.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: A source of nitrogen input, though small in this context.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric N2 into ammonia by microorganisms.

entities.name: BACTERIA
entities.description: Microorganisms associated with root systems involved in nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: BLUE-GREEN ALGAL CRUSTS
entities.description: Surface-dwelling organisms involved in biological nitrogen fixation.
